updated_conclusion: The worker payload activator at 0x14066E030 builds a small callback wrapper around 0x14066F3F0 through shared constructor 0x1407CD29C, then installs the resulting ref-counted pair through 0x1400A8090. The callback itself marks the payload active, waits/pumps the worker payload state, appends the wrapper record into the parent object's +0xE0 active-entry vector under the +0x90 lock, marks the payload published, releases the temporary pair, and frees the 0x20-byte wrapper.

tooling_milestone.change: Added a lightweight native x64 instruction-window exporter with call and branch target annotation, .pdata function attribution, ModRM memory/register formatting, common MSVC integer/SSE opcodes, and raw-byte fallback for unsupported opcodes.
tooling_milestone.why_it_matters: The callback/wrapper path needed exact instruction windows but no longer requires a Ghidra headless dump for every short helper body. The RS2 windows in this milestone decode with zero unknown instructions.

address_refs.role: First branch passes 0x14066F3F0 as a callback/code pointer into wrapper construction.
Second branch passes the same callback/code pointer into the sibling wrapper construction path.

ordered_calls.role: Initializes or wakes the object referenced by wrapper+0x18.
Performs a small scheduler/state helper call using ECX=0x10 and EDX=0.
Pumps or waits the payload while bytes +0xD8/+0xD9/+0xDC are not in a terminal state.
Locks parent+0x90 before appending the active wrapper entry.
Appends or inserts wrapper+0x8 into the parent+0xE0 active-entry vector.
Frees or returns the 0x20-byte wrapper allocation.
Failure path after parent+0x90 lock acquisition reports non-zero.

observed_behavior: Treats RCX as the small wrapper object; reads parent from [RCX], payload from [RCX+0x10], and an auxiliary object from [RCX+0x18].
Loops through the payload vtable +0x8 while [payload+0xD9] is clear and payload flags +0xD8/+0xDC do not force exit.
On the successful lock path, appends wrapper+0x8 to parent+0xE0, sets [payload+0xDB] to 1, unlocks parent+0x90, releases wrapper+0x8, and frees the wrapper itself.
The callback has no direct CALL rel32 callers; it is used as a code pointer by 0x14066E030.
callback_pointer_0x14066f3f0.confidence: medium-high for fields/calls/lifetime; medium for exact scheduler semantics

observed_behavior: Returns null through an error/assert path when R8 is null.
Calls 0x1407CD238 with RCX=R8 and RDX=R9; the result is held in RBX and used as the constructed backing object.
Calls an indirect import at 0x140802328 with RCX=caller output/root, RDX=the EDX flags value, R8=0x1407CD164, R9=RBX, plus stack arguments, producing the wrapper pointer.
On wrapper construction failure, releases RBX+0x10 and RBX+0x18 through imported release functions and then calls 0x1407DCC3C on RBX.
On success, optionally writes the stack result dword back to the caller-provided out pointer and returns the wrapper pointer.
wrapper_constructor_0x1407cd29c.confidence: medium-high for call shape and ownership cleanup; medium for exact imported allocator/runtime API names

observed_behavior: Reads two qwords from source RDX, zeros the source pair, and writes the pair into destination RCX.
If the destination previously held a non-null first qword, releases it with interlocked decrements at +0x8/+0xC and vtable calls +0x8/+0x10.
Returns the destination pointer in RAX.

descriptor_pointer_0x140b9fcc8.bounded_inference: The same two activator branches that pass callback 0x14066F3F0 also load descriptor/data pointer 0x140B9FCC8, tying the callback wrapper to the worker payload/container descriptor previously observed in 0x14066E030.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.worker-validation-container-and-bootstra: Names the callback and pair-installer side of 0x14066E030, which was previously only identified as a worker payload/container activator.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.worker-sorted-vector-helper-map: Explains how the active-entry vector at parent+0xE0 is fed before cleanup later removes keyed records from parent+0x70.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.reverser-tooling: Turns the manual Ghidra instruction-window step into a native Reverser capability for future short-helper tracing.

next_targets: Trace 0x1401369D0 to name the parent+0xE0 vector append helper and align it with cleanup path 0x14066D7B4.
Trace 0x1407A4620/0x1407A6630/0x1407A6990 enough to name the scheduler side effects inside 0x14066F3F0.
Trace 0x14066D010, 0x1400D24B0, and 0x1400D1990 to connect constructor-side worker labels to the provider objects that reach this callback-wrapper path.
